items.title: Albanese will today try to defend his prime-time message

children.content: With no realistic end in sight and a whole lot of economic pain still to come, the PM's decision to make a direct plea to the nation was not one taken lightly.

gallery.altText: A man in a suit looks across the camera with an australian flag in the background.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a suit looks across the camera with an australian flag in the background.

items.title: One important man was missing from Albanese's national address

children.content: The prime minister risks criticism for using the rare measure of a national address to urge calm without announcing any new measures.

image.altText: Albanese, standing in an office and wearing a navy suit and light blue tie, looks at the camera

items.title: Trump says he's considering pulling US out of 'paper tiger' NATO

children.content: Donald Trump says he is strongly considering pulling the United States out of NATO after allies failed to back US military action against Iran, in an interview with the UK's Daily Telegraph.

image.altText: A man in a suit and tie sits at a desk

items.title: Iran war may depend on who runs out of missiles and interceptors first

children.content: Analysts estimate the US is burning through high-end weapons in Iran at a rate that could take years to replenish, risking the West's military readiness for other potential conflicts.

image.altText: A missile launch from the front of a military vessel in the ocean.

items.title: One Nation picked candidates accused of domestic abuse, antisemitism

children.content: A One Nation candidate in the South Australian election was accused of domestic abuse, bullying and harassment, while another made racist, antisemitic and homophobic social media posts, raising fresh questions about the party's vetting processes as it surges in popularity.

image.altText: A combination of two images, each showing a man in a suit smiling in front of a soft green backdrop.

items.title: Next generation heads for the Moon

children.content: If all goes well for NASA's Artemis II mission, the astronauts aboard could fly the furthest that humans have ever gone. What should we be looking out for?

gallery.altText: A 3D wireframe rocket with 3 male and 1 female astronauts pose with looming large moon behind

thumbnail.altText: A 3D wireframe rocket with 3 male and 1 female astronauts pose with looming large moon behind

items.title: Almost 50 French cities have free public transport. What can Australia learn?

children.content: There are growing calls in Australia for free public transport amid the Iran war with two states putting in place free services on a temporary basis. But in France, almost 50 cities have free public transport.

image.altText: Montpellier tramway with a message promoting free public transport.

items.title: 'Dance for me': Australian Harrods survivor tells of Mohamed Al Fayed abuse

children.content: Jessica tried to keep her distance from Harrods billionaire owner Mohamed Al Fayed, who was known to abuse his female staff, until he spotted her going down an escalator.

image.altText: A woman looks directly at the camera.

items.title: Singapore's biggest oil source is blocked and experts warn Australians will pay

children.content: Singapore is exposed to the oil shock from Iran’s effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz, and its response will shape the fuel crisis in Australia.

image.altText: Chimneys and white storage tankers next to a bay of grey water.

items.title: Labor calls for investigation into Hellyer mine allegations

children.content: Labor has called on the Tasmanian government to investigate allegations a metals mine, when under a previous ownership, was being used by organised crime affiliates to launder money.

image.altText: A close up of mining truck wheels and a large tunnel.

items.title: Keep track of today's sharp drop in petrol prices around the country

children.content: The federal government's cut to fuel excise has almost immediately flowed through to petrol prices at the pump, according to ABC tracking of fuel price data nationwide.

items.title: Weather to deliver Easter treat, but another cyclone looms next week

children.content: Australia has just been soaked by its third wettest February-March on record, but for most of the country, skies have cleared up just in time for Easter.

image.altText: Close up of small easter eggs in a bowl.

items.title: Parents demand action after concrete fell into Melbourne classroom

children.content: Collingwood College is sending students to another campus for science classes after concrete fell from its water-damaged roof and forced the closure of an entire floor of its main building.

image.altText: Concrete from roof of Collingwood College

items.title: The big gamble Andy Griffiths took to become a world-famous kids' author

children.content: Andy Griffiths has written 43 books, is the Children's Laureate, and is about to turn his Treehouse series into an ABC TV show. So what inspires the former punk and teacher to keep creating quirky tales for kids?

gallery.altText: Andy Griffiths smiles with his tattooed arms crossed

thumbnail.altText: Andy Griffiths smiles with his tattooed arms crossed

avatar.alt: Virginia Trioli in a stylish suit on a set with lighting behind her

items.title: Museum fans: Test yourself with our curious collection of trivia

children.content: Australia’s museums hold a treasure trove of odd facts, native animals and even burlesque history. So how much do you know about the things hidden in our nation’s vaults?

image.altText: A close up of a plastic dinosaur.

items.title: Study shows gen Z craves in-person connection more than other generations

children.content: Gen Z is embracing retro technology like iPods, vinyl and Polaroid cameras and craves in-person connection more than any other generation, according to a Live Nation study.

image.altText: A young woman with long blonde hair wearing a pink and black cap, pink shirt, rolling her eyes

items.title: Arnold carried in jubilant scenes after Iraq qualifies for World Cup

children.content: Former Socceroos coach Graham Arnold has guided Iraq to their first FIFA World Cup appearance in 40 years with a playoff win over Bolivia.

gallery.altText: Iraq's coach Graham Arnold carried by players after qualifying for FIFA World Cup, massive crowd waiving flags

thumbnail.altText: Iraq's coach Graham Arnold carried by players after qualifying for FIFA World Cup, massive crowd waiving flags

items.title: WA man charged with terrorism offences wrote of Bondi attacks in diary, court told

children.content: Jayson Joseph Michaels, 20, had reflected on the Bondi attacks in his diary and was allegedly planning a "day of justice", a Perth court hears.

image.altText: A smiling youg man in high-vis in front of a digger holding a hose.

items.title: Staff accused of stealing $1m from Village Roadshow

children.content: Village Roadshow allege more than $1 million in inappropriate payments had already been identified "with the total amount likely to increase as the investigation continues".

image.altText: people on rollercoaster

items.title: Former Sydney University lecturer seen in Iran video about Hormuz Strait

children.content: An Australian academic has appeared in an Iranian propaganda video supporting a toll system imposed by the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps on foreign merchant shipping.

image.altText: A man gives the peace sign with his hand.

items.title: Robotaxi malfunction in China causes traffic chaos as cars stall

children.content: Videos on social media showed cars stopped in the middle of multi-lane roads with their hazard lights on obstructing traffic.

image.altText: Two cars with hazard lights on stall on a highway.

items.title: At least four killed in daytime Russian drone strikes across Ukraine

children.content: Both sides are launching drone attacks as the war between Russia and Ukraine drags into its fifth year.

image.altText: Firefighters on a roof spraying water onto a large blaze on a neighbouring building.

items.title: Sixers force NBL title decider after beating Kings in cliffhanger

children.content: Torrey Craig's missed free throw for the Sydney Kings in the dying seconds allows the Adelaide 36ers to level the NBL championship series at 2-2 with a gripping 92-91 win.

image.altText: Two basketball players in action, one in a white uniform and one in a navy uniform

items.title: Cultural compensation replaces fine in 'rare' illegal logging ruling

children.content: Traditional owners say a $450,000 fine will not replace the six centuries-old giant trees illegally felled by NSW Forestry Corporation.

gallery.altText: An aerial view of a forest with a river running through it and falling down a waterfall.

thumbnail.altText: An aerial view of a forest with a river running through it and falling down a waterfall.

items.title: Bluey's lead composer on helping kids fall in love with music

children.content: A new orchestral Bluey album is out. Lead composer Joff Bush talks about classical music, storytelling and inspiring kids to love music.

image.altText: A man in a colourful shirt plays the melodica

items.title: Dire future of superb fairy-wren could mean catastrophe for other species

children.content: The researchers said the superb fairy-wren would likely go extinct within 30-40 years unless action was taken to stop greenhouse gas emissions causing climate change.

image.altText: A small blue-headed wren sitting in a tree.

items.title: Crucial exhibits unveiled to jury in Irma Palasics cold case trial

children.content: A milk container and water jug carrying DNA evidence were revealed to jurors as a decades-old murder trial reached its final stage.

image.altText: A fridge with many containers in it.

items.title: Australia's solar panel waste issue is growing in size year-on-year

children.content: Australians are expected to throw away 90,000 tonnes of solar panel waste each year by 2030, a federal government hearing has been told.

image.altText: Four rows of dark solar panels on a silver roof.

items.title: 'Nothing's out here' Vodafone ad angers rural Australians

children.content: A new Vodafone campaign has regional Australians seething after the telecommunications giant claimed to focus its network infrastructure on where "98 whatever per cent of you actually live".

gallery.altText: A screengrab of an ad campaign featuring an emu standing in the outback next to a woman in a red dress

thumbnail.altText: A screengrab of an ad campaign featuring an emu standing in the outback next to a woman in a red dress

items.title: Share markets energised as they price in end to Iran war, but there's a catch

children.content: If you had woken up today and heard someone say Wall Street's tech sector rose more than 3 per cent overnight, you would expect it to be promptly followed by "April Fools' Day".

image.altText: A trader looks up at a chart on his computer screen while working on the floor of the New York Stock Exchange.

items.title: NBL owner prepared to walk away from major land purchase

children.content: After the Tasmanian government was urged to abandon the sale of prime waterfront land but chose to persevere, it now appears the developer is prepared to "walk away" from the potential purchase.

image.altText: An undeveloped area of waterfront land in front of a city, beyond which looms an escarpment.

items.title: Fears for stingray populations as algal bloom moves north

children.content: Evidence of South Australia's harmful algal bloom has been detected in previously untouched parts of the Spencer Gulf days after rays were seen gathering in shallow waters.

image.altText: A dead stingray lying in a small puddle among rocks, with its white belly facing up

items.title: 'We are an expert': How Lebanon's airport keeps running despite Israeli strikes

children.content: Beirut-Rafic Hariri Airport, Lebanon's gateway to the world, is still operating even as Israel bombards its surroundings.

image.altText: An airport hall with lined floor, seats, a glass ceiling, and no people around

items.title: Meet the Artemis II crew who are heading to the Moon

children.content: The Artemis II mission to the Moon could take four astronauts further from Earth than any human has gone before. Here's what we know about them.

gallery.altText: Four astronauts in blue NASA suits speaking while their rocket is behind them in the distance

thumbnail.altText: Four astronauts in blue NASA suits speaking while their rocket is behind them in the distance
